Patch Set 3:

Amir, if you are resubmitting these patches, you need to base them on the latest tip of the master branch.  These patches are currently based on commit v2_4_93_0-48-ge789a35 "LU-4024 tests: cleanup file system before DNE tests" which was landed 2 months ago.  It looks like the tests are failing due to several old bugs, and even if they did pass there is a good chance there would be patch conflicts if they tried to land.

Uploaded patch set 10.

Patch Set 10:

Comments on handling the ping info:

A suggested solution is to create a new ping info that contains the new information and replace the pointer of the md from the old pointer to the new pointer.  This will require some changes in the md infrastructure.

so basically we'd have:
lnet_replace_ni_ping_info(ni)
  . create a new lnet_ping_info_t
  . which will have the new information after the addition or removal of the ni.
  . convert the lnet_ping_info md handle to an md and replace the pointer to the start of the ping info, to the new one, and delete the old one.

This will require some changes to the md infrastructure, to be able to make this modification.

thoughts

I'm still looking at this patch, more comments may come, but here is a serious issue.

Patch Set 12:

Amir, I did a quick look of this patch but didn't quite understand why we need a new mutex ln_ping_info_mutex, I can't see any operation has to be protected by sleep lock mutex instead of spinlock, current LNet already has complex lock mechanism and many locks, would it be possible reuse any of those locks? 
more over, if ping_info has to be regenerated while adding/removing NI, we probably should give ping_info refcount instead of having single copy. So we can put old copies on deathrow list, and create a new ping_info right away, and avoid all kinds of blocking/locking issues?

Patch Set 15:

(21 comments)

I made an extra change to allow the configuration of networks without having to do "lctl network up", which was the intended design.  The change is to process IOC_LIBCFS_ADD_NET in lnet_ioctl().  Had to also remove a couple of asserts, which are no expected, namely asserts on the_lnet.ln_refcount > 0

Patch Set 21:

It was pointed out to me that this patch series is based on a very old version of master - commit v2_5_53_0-1-g5764953, which was landed on 2014/01/02.  There have been 375 files changed, 31670 insertions(+), 18102 deletions(-) since that time, so a high likelihood that this patch series would not be able to land to master, even after spending time to inspect it and pass all of the testing.

Further, there is a danger that there will need to be code restructuring due to more than just context changes due to other features being landed in the meantime (though fortunately since LNET is relatively static, there is less danger of this).

Finally, this is causing builds to take a very long time because the kernel that this patch is based on is different than all of the other patches being built today, and therefore requires a full kernel rebuild, increasing the build time from 30 minutes to 3h.

Please rebase the patches to the tip of master as you refresh them due to patch inspections, so that there is at least some chance they can land after inspection and testing have finished.

Patch Set 21: Verified-1

parents.subject: LU-3952 nfs: don't panic NFS server if MDS fails to find FID

commit.subject: LU-2456 lnet: Dynamic LNet Configuration (DLC) Feature
commit.message:
LU-2456 lnet: Dynamic LNet Configuration (DLC) Feature

This is the first patch of a set of patches that enables DLC.

This patch adds the following features to LNET.  Currently these
features are not driven by user space.
- Enabling Routing on Demand
- Adjusting router buffer pools
- Adding/deleting routes dynamically
- Adding/Deleting Networks dynamically

Signed-off-by: Amir Shehata <amir.shehata@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ifec040ba2dc6ac4898be2542d896298621901387
